content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN).

A simple path between two different vertices aaa and bbb (a<ba \lt ba<b) is called a clamp path if it does not contain any vertices with numbers smaller then aaa or greater than bbb.

Calculate the number of clamp paths in the given tree.
constraints: 0≤pi≤N0 \leq p_i \leq N0≤pi​≤N for each valid iii
there is exactly one vertex iii with pi=0p_i = 0pi​=0
the graph described on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​. For each valid iii, if pi≠0p_i \neq 0pi​=0, then there is an edge between vertices iii and pip_ipi​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer - the number of clamp paths.
